You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[0]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_0.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 0: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""A/B CERTIFIER for the PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS map-then-reduce port (this worktree).
2	
3	Proves the parallel path (_compose_section_per_basket with workers=16) produces the BYTE-IDENTICAL
4	ordered output list as the serial path (workers=1) on the SAME per-basket producer outputs — i.e. the
5	reconciled MAP/REDUCE dedup is verdict-set-identical to this worktree's serial loop, and has no
6	shared-state race under real thread interleaving.
7	
8	Method: monkeypatch the per-basket producers the MAP calls with DETERMINISTIC-per-basket functions
9	(same basket -> same text in both arms) that inject a RANDOM sleep (forces the ThreadPool to interleave
10	in a different order each call). The producer outputs are crafted to hit every REDUCE branch:
11	  - unique units (kept)
12	  - exact duplicate text+span of an earlier basket (subset+text dedup -> dropped)
13	  - same footprint, NO new number (footprint-number collapse -> dropped)
14	  - same footprint, NEW number (survives)
15	  - internal §3.5 insufficient-evidence marker (whole basket skipped, aux discarded)
16	  - aux units (companion/qualifier/distinct) incl. an aux that duplicates a main (aux dedup)
17	If serial != parallel on ANY of 200 randomized trials, the port has a race. Zero LLM.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import os
22	import random
23	import sys
24	import time
25	
26	os.environ["PG_COMPANION_FIGURE_COMPOSE"] = "1"
27	os.environ["PG_QUALIFIER_ELABORATION"] = "1"
28	os.environ["PG_SUBTOPIC_ADDITIVE_FACTS"] = "1"
29	
30	import src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose as vc  # noqa: E402
31	
32	MARKER = "[insufficient verified evidence for this basket]"
33	
34	
35	def _tok(ev: str, s: int, e: int) -> str:
36	    return f"[#ev:{ev}:{s}-{e}]"
37	
38	
39	# Deterministic per-basket MAIN text. Baskets are ints 0..N-1.
40	def _main_text(b: int) -> str:
41	    if b == 3:
42	        return MARKER  # §3.5: whole basket (incl aux) must be skipped
43	    if b == 5:
44	        # exact duplicate of basket 0's text+span -> subset+text dedup drop
45	        return f"Finding zero {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rate 5%."
46	    if b == 8:
47	        # same footprint as basket 0 (ev0:0-10) but NO new number token beyond '5%' -> collapse
48	        return f"Restated finding {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rate 5%."
49	    if b == 9:
50	        # same footprint as basket 0 but a NEW number (12%) -> survives
51	        return f"Extended finding {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rose to 12%."
52	    if b == 0:
53	        return f"Finding zero {_tok('ev0', 0, 10)} rate 5%."
54	    return f"Finding {b} {_tok(f'ev{b}', b, b + 20)} value {b}0%."
55	
56	
57	def _aux_texts(b: int) -> list[str]:
58	    outs = []
59	    if b in (2, 6):
60	        outs.append(f"Companion {b} {_tok(f'evc{b}', 0, 15)} extra {b}1%.")
61	    if b == 6:
62	        # aux that duplicates basket 6's own companion -> aux dedup drop
63	        outs.append(f"Companion 6 {_tok('evc6', 0, 15)} extra 61%.")
64	    if b in (4, 7):
65	        outs.append(f"Qualifier {b} {_tok(f'evq{b}', 5, 40)} scope {b}.")
66	    return outs
67	
68	
69	def _sleep():
70	    time.sleep(random.uniform(0, 0.004))
71	
72	
73	def _install_stubs():
74	    vc._distinct_origin_supports = lambda basket: []  # force single-basket path (no multicited)
75	
76	    def _stub_compose_one_basket(basket, evidence_pool, **kw):
77	        _sleep()
78	        return _main_text(basket)
79	
80	    vc._compose_one_basket = _stub_compose_one_basket
81	
82	    def _stub_companion(basket, evidence_pool, composed, **kw):
83	        _sleep()
84	        return [t for t in _aux_texts(basket) if t.startswith("Companion")]
85	
86	    def _stub_qualifier(basket, evidence_pool, composed, **kw):
87	        _sleep()
88	        return [t for t in _aux_texts(basket) if t.startswith("Qualifier")]
89	
90	    def _stub_distinct(basket, evidence_pool, composed, **kw):
91	        _sleep()
92	        return []
93	
94	    vc.compose_companion_figure_units = _stub_companion
95	    vc.compose_qualifier_elaboration_units = _stub_qualifier
96	    vc.compose_distinct_fact_units = _stub_distinct
97	
98	
99	def _run(workers: int, baskets: list[int]) -> list[str]:
100	    os.environ["PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS"] = str(workers)
101	    return vc._compose_section_per_basket(
102	        baskets, {}, writer_fn=lambda b, p: "", verify_fn=lambda *a, **k: None,
103	    )
104	
105	
106	def main() -> int:
107	    _install_stubs()
108	    baskets = list(range(40))
109	    serial = _run(1, baskets)
110	    # sanity: the serial path exercised real dedup (dropped at least the crafted duplicates)
111	    print(f"serial units kept = {len(serial)} (of {len(baskets)} baskets + aux)")
112	    assert MARKER not in "\n".join(serial), "§3.5 marker leaked"
113	    assert not any("Finding 3 " in u for u in serial), "basket 3 (marker) should be fully skipped"
114	
115	    trials = 200
116	    fails = 0
117	    for i in range(trials):
118	        random.seed(1000 + i)
119	        par = _run(16, baskets)
120	        if par != serial:
121	            fails += 1
122	            if fails <= 3:
123	                print(f"  DIVERGENCE trial {i}: serial={len(serial)} par={len(par)}")
124	                for a, b in zip(serial, par):
125	                    if a != b:
126	                        print(f"    serial: {a!r}\n    par   : {b!r}")
127	                        break
128	    if fails:
129	        print(f"FAIL: {fails}/{trials} parallel trials DIVERGED from serial")
130	        return 1
131	    print(f"PASS: {trials}/{trials} parallel (workers=16) trials BYTE-IDENTICAL to serial (workers=1)")
132	    print("VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: IDENTICAL under randomized thread interleaving")
133	    return 0
134	
135	
136	if __name__ == "__main__":
137	    sys.exit(main())
138

1	"""
2	Compose-stage validation using OpenAI as substitute for OpenRouter (which is 402 blocked).
3	
4	PURPOSE: validate the LLM compose path of `compose_from_wiki()` end-to-end.
5	This is the ONE pipeline stage we have not exercised on real LLM output.
6	
7	WHAT THIS PROVES:
8	- The wiki structure feeds correctly into the composer
9	- The LLM honors [REF:N] citations with the 5-lens scaffold
10	- _scrub_cot() runs cleanly on real model output
11	- [REF:N] -> [N] resolution works on real prose
12	- Final report assembles with bibliography
13	- Quality gate evaluates correctly
14	
15	WHAT THIS DOES NOT PROVE:
16	- Final G-Eval score (model is gpt-4o-mini, not Qwen 3.5 Plus)
17	- Real LLM evidence extraction quality from raw markdown
18	- Production timeout behavior at 600-evidence scale
19	
20	This is a mechanical-pipeline validator, not a quality benchmark.
21	"""
22	import asyncio
23	import os
24	import re
25	import sys
26	import time
27	from dataclasses import dataclass
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Any, Optional
30	
31	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
32	
33	from dotenv import load_dotenv
34	load_dotenv()
35	
36	os.environ["PG_WIKI_ENABLED"] = "1"
37	os.environ["PG_WIKI_5LENS"] = "1"
38	
39	import logging
40	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(message)s")
41	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
42	
43	
44	# ── OpenAI shim that mimics OpenRouterClient.generate() ──────────────
45	
46	@dataclass
47	class _ShimResponse:
48	    content: str
49	    reasoning: Optional[str] = None
50	    input_tokens: int = 0
51	    output_tokens: int = 0
52	    reasoning_tokens: int = 0
53	    model: str = "gpt-4o-mini"
54	    duration_ms: float = 0
55	    raw_response: Optional[dict] = None
56	
57	
58	class OpenAIShimClient:
59	    """Minimal OpenAI client with the OpenRouterClient.generate() interface.
60	
61	    Defaults to gpt-5 (the strongest available reasoning model). gpt-5 quirks:
62	    - Uses `max_completion_tokens`, NOT `max_tokens`
63	    - Only supports default temperature=1.0 (rejects custom temp)
64	    - Burns reasoning tokens BEFORE producing content — needs generous budget
65	    """
66	
67	    # Models that share gpt-5's reasoning quirks (flagged for param handling)
68	    _REASONING_MODELS = {"gpt-5", "o3", "o3-mini", "o1", "o1-preview", "o1-mini"}
69	
70	    def __init__(self, model: str = "gpt-5"):
71	        import httpx
72	        self.model = model
73	        self.is_reasoning = any(model.startswith(m) for m in self._REASONING_MODELS)
74	        self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(
75	            base_url="https://api.openai.com/v1",
76	            headers={
77	                "Authorization": f"Bearer {os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY', '')}",
78	                "Content-Type": "application/json",
79	            },
80	            timeout=httpx.Timeout(300.0, connect=30.0),
81	        )
82	        self.calls = 0
83	        self.total_input = 0
84	        self.total_output = 0
85	        self.total_reasoning = 0
86	
87	    async def close(self):
88	        await self._client.aclose()
89	
90	    async def generate(
91	        self,
92	        prompt: str,
93	        system: str = "",
94	        max_tokens: int = 4096,
95	        temperature: float = 0.3,
96	        timeout: Optional[float] = None,
97	        **kwargs,
98	    ) -> _ShimResponse:
99	        messages = []
100	        if system:
101	            messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system})
102	        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
103	
104	        body: dict[str, Any] = {
105	            "model": self.model,
106	            "messages": messages,
107	        }
108	
109	        if self.is_reasoning:
110	            # gpt-5/o3 burn reasoning tokens before content. Triple the budget
111	            # so reasoning doesn't starve the prose output.
112	            body["max_completion_tokens"] = max(max_tokens * 3, 8000)
113	            # No temperature — gpt-5 only accepts default 1.0
114	        else:
115	            body["max_tokens"] = max_tokens
116	            body["temperature"] = temperature
117	
118	        start = time.monotonic()
119	        resp = await self._client.post("/chat/completions", json=body)
120	        resp.raise_for_status()
121	        data = resp.json()
122	        duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000
123	
124	        content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"] or ""
125	        usage = data.get("usage", {}) or {}
126	        in_tok = usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0)
127	        out_tok = usage.get("completion_tokens", 0)
128	        rsn_tok = (usage.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}).get("reasoning_tokens", 0)
129	
130	        self.calls += 1
131	        self.total_input += in_tok
132	        self.total_output += out_tok
133	        self.total_reasoning += rsn_tok
134	
135	        return _ShimResponse(
136	            content=content,
137	            input_tokens=in_tok,
138	            output_tokens=out_tok,
139	            reasoning_tokens=rsn_tok,
140	            model=self.model,
141	            duration_ms=duration_ms,
142	        )
143	
144	
145	# ── Build a small validated wiki slice ───────────────────────────────
146	
147	def _make_evidence(url: str, title: str, statement: str, quote: str,
148	                   tier: str = "GOLD", auth: float = 0.85,

150	    import hashlib

220	        ),

222	            "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0002916523063320",
223	            "TRE Effect on Apolipoprotein B (AJCN 2023)",
224	            "TRE reduced apoB by 7.4 mg/dL (95% CI -12.8 to -2.0) and improved insulin sensitivity by 24%.",
225	            "TRE significantly reduced apoB by 7.4 mg/dL (95% CI -12.8 to -2.0) and improved insulin sensitivity (HOMA-IR) by 24% versus controls.",

233	        ),

236	            "IF and Eating Disorder Risk (Nature Med 2022)",
237	            "Intermittent fasting was associated with 1.5x higher binge-eating risk (OR 1.49, 95% CI 1.21-1.84) in young adults.",
238	            "In a cohort of 2762 young adults, intermittent fasting was associated with higher binge-eating disorder risk (adjusted OR 1.49, 95% CI 1.21-1.84).",
239	        ),

241	            "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35635175/",
242	            "Adverse Events in IF Trials Meta-analysis",

244	            "Across 18 trials reporting adverse events, the most common were headache (12% of participants), fatigue (8%), and irritability (6%).",

255	def _outline() -> list[dict]:
256	    return [
257	        {"section_id": "s01", "title": "Weight Loss and Body Composition",
258	         "description": "Effect of intermittent fasting protocols on body weight, fat mass, and adherence."},
259	        {"section_id": "s02", "title": "Cardiovascular and Lipid Health",
260	         "description": "Cardiovascular outcomes including lipid profile, blood pressure, and mortality risk."},

263	    ]

266	# ── Main validation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
267	
268	async def main():
269	    if not os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"):
270	        print("ERROR: OPENAI_API_KEY not set in .env")
271	        return 1
272	
273	    model_name = os.getenv("OPENAI_TEST_MODEL", "gpt-5")
274	    print("=" * 70)
275	    print(f"WIKI COMPOSE VALIDATION via OpenAI shim ({model_name})")
276	    print("=" * 70)
277	
278	    # ── Stage 1: Build wiki from seed evidence ───────────────────────
279	    print("\n[1/4] Building wiki from 13 seed evidence pieces")
280	    from src.polaris_graph.wiki.wiki_builder import build_wiki
281	
282	    evidence = _seed_evidence()
283	    outline = _outline()
284	    print(f"  Evidence: {len(evidence)} pieces")
285	    print(f"  Outline:  {len(outline)} sections")
286	
287	    wiki = build_wiki(
288	        evidence=evidence, outline=outline,
289	        query="intermittent fasting health benefits and risks",
290	        vector_id="OPENAI_COMPOSE_VALIDATION",
291	    )
292	
293	    total_claims = sum(len(c) for c in wiki.section_claims.values())
294	    print(f"  Built wiki: {total_claims} claims, {len(wiki.bibliography)} bib entries")
295	    for sid, claims in wiki.section_claims.items():
296	        sec_title = next((s["title"] for s in outline if s["section_id"] == sid), sid)
297	        print(f"    {sid}: {len(claims):2d} claims | {sec_title[:45]}")
298	
299	    if total_claims == 0:
300	        print("\nFAIL: wiki has no claims — cannot proceed")
301	        return 1
302	
303	    # ── Stage 2: Compose with OpenAI shim ────────────────────────────
304	    print(f"\n[2/4] Composing report via OpenAI shim ({model_name})")
305	    from src.polaris_graph.wiki.wiki_composer import compose_from_wiki
306	
307	    client = OpenAIShimClient(model=model_name)
308	    start = time.monotonic()
309	    try:
310	        result = await compose_from_wiki(
311	            client=client,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
312	            wiki_result=wiki,
313	            query="intermittent fasting health benefits and risks",
314	            outline=outline,
315	        )
316	    except Exception as exc:
317	        print(f"\nFAIL: compose_from_wiki raised: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
318	        await client.close()
319	        return 1
320	
321	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
322	    await client.close()
323	
324	    # ── Stage 3: Inspect output ─────────────────────────────────────
325	    print(f"\n[3/4] Compose complete in {elapsed:.0f}s ({client.calls} LLM calls)")
326	    print(f"  Status: {result['status']}")
327	    print(f"  Quality gate: {result['quality_gate_result']}")
328	
329	    sections = result["sections"]
330	    qm = result["quality_metrics"]
331	    print(f"\n  Sections composed: {len(sections)}/{len(outline)}")
332	    print(f"  Total words:       {qm['total_words']}")
333	    print(f"  Total citations:   {qm['total_citations']}")
334	    print(f"  Unique sources:    {qm['unique_sources']}")
335	    print(f"  Zero-cite sec:     {qm['zero_cite_sections']}")
336	    print(f"  Avg cite/sec:      {qm['avg_citations_per_section']:.1f}")
337	
338	    for s in sections:
339	        print(f"    {s['section_id']}: {s['word_count']:4d} words, "
340	              f"{len(s['citation_ids']):2d} unique cites — {s['title'][:40]}")
341	
342	    # ── Stage 4: Validation checks ──────────────────────────────────
343	    print(f"\n[4/4] Validation checks")
344	    checks = []
345	
346	    # V1: All sections produced content
347	    v1 = len(sections) == len(outline)
348	    checks.append(("V1 all sections composed",
349	                   v1, f"{len(sections)}/{len(outline)}"))
350	
351	    # V2: No CoT leakage (look for telltale phrases)
352	    cot_phrases = ["let me", "i need to", "first, i'll", "thinking about",
353	                   "let's start", "i should", "i will write", "okay,",
354	                   "as an ai", "i'll write", "here's a"]
355	    cot_hits = []
356	    for s in sections:
357	        low = s["content"].lower()
358	        for ph in cot_phrases:
359	            if ph in low:
360	                cot_hits.append((s["section_id"], ph))
361	    v2 = len(cot_hits) == 0
362	    checks.append(("V2 no CoT leakage", v2,
363	                   f"{len(cot_hits)} hits" if cot_hits else "clean"))
364	
365	    # V3: All citation prefixes resolved → bare [N].
366	    # The model intermittently emits [REF:N], [CITE:N], or [Ref:N].
367	    leftover_pattern = re.compile(r"\[(?:REF|CITE|Ref|Cite|ref|cite):\d+\]")
368	    leftover_refs = []
369	    for s in sections:
370	        if leftover_pattern.search(s["content"]):
371	            leftover_refs.append(s["section_id"])
372	    # Also check the abstract via final_report
373	    abstract_leak = bool(leftover_pattern.search(result.get("final_report", "")))
374	    if abstract_leak:
375	        leftover_refs.append("abstract")
376	    v3 = len(leftover_refs) == 0
377	    checks.append(("V3 citation prefixes resolved",
378	                   v3, f"{len(leftover_refs)} leaks" if leftover_refs else "clean"))
379	
380	    # V3b: No literal [N] placeholder (model writing template literal as citation)
381	    placeholder_hits = []
382	    if re.search(r"\[N\]", result.get("final_report", "")):
383	        placeholder_hits.append("final_report")
384	    for s in sections:
385	        if re.search(r"\[N\]", s["content"]):
386	            placeholder_hits.append(s["section_id"])
387	    v3b = len(placeholder_hits) == 0
388	    checks.append(("V3b no literal [N] placeholder",
389	                   v3b, f"{len(placeholder_hits)} leaks" if placeholder_hits else "clean"))
390	
391	    # V4: Citation density relative to the available source pool.
392	    # Absolute ceiling = (unique_sources / total_words) * 100. With only 13
393	    # seed sources and ~2000 words, the ceiling is ~0.65/100w. We require
394	    # the model to hit at least 80% of that ceiling, i.e. it must use most
395	    # of the available sources rather than recycling a few. At production
396	    # scale (200 sources / 10K words) this becomes 0.8 * 2.0 = 1.6/100w.
397	    avg_density = (qm["total_citations"] / qm["total_words"] * 100) if qm["total_words"] else 0
398	    ceiling = (qm["unique_sources"] / qm["total_words"] * 100) if qm["total_words"] else 0
399	    target_density = ceiling * 0.8
400	    v4 = avg_density >= target_density
401	    checks.append(("V4 citation density vs pool",
402	                   v4, f"{avg_density:.2f}/100w vs target {target_density:.2f} "
403	                        f"(ceiling {ceiling:.2f})"))
404	
405	    # V5: Every numbered citation in body exists in bibliography
406	    bib_refs = set(b["ref_num"] for b in wiki.bibliography)
407	    orphans = []
408	    for s in sections:
409	        nums = set(int(n) for n in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", s["content"]))
410	        for n in nums:
411	            if n not in bib_refs:
412	                orphans.append((s["section_id"], n))
413	    v5 = len(orphans) == 0
414	    checks.append(("V5 no orphan citations",
415	                   v5, f"{len(orphans)} orphans" if orphans else "0 orphans"))
416	
417	    # V6: Final report assembled with bibliography
418	    final = result["final_report"]
419	    v6 = (
420	        "## References" in final
421	        and "[1]" in final
422	        and len(final) > 1000
423	    )
424	    checks.append(("V6 final report assembled",
425	                   v6, f"{len(final)} chars"))
426	
427	    # V7: Word count gate (relaxed for 3-section test)
428	    v7 = qm["total_words"] >= 1500
429	    checks.append(("V7 word count >= 1500",
430	                   v7, f"{qm['total_words']} words"))
431	
432	    # Print results
433	    print()
434	    all_pass = True
435	    for name, ok, detail in checks:
436	        marker = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
437	        if not ok:
438	            all_pass = False
439	        print(f"  [{marker}] {name:30s} {detail}")
440	
441	    # ── Save artifacts ──────────────────────────────────────────────
442	    out_dir = Path("outputs/polaris_graph")
443	    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
444	    out_file = out_dir / "OPENAI_COMPOSE_VALIDATION.md"
445	    out_file.write_text(final, encoding="utf-8")
446	    print(f"\nReport saved: {out_file} ({len(final)} chars)")
447

449	    # Pricing per million tokens (rough estimates)

451	        "gpt-5":         (1.25, 10.00),
452	        "gpt-5-mini":    (0.25,  2.00),
453	        "o3":            (15.00, 60.00),
454	        "o3-mini":       ( 3.00, 12.00),

458	    }

460	    cost = (client.total_input / 1_000_000 * in_p) + (client.total_output / 1_000_000 * out_p)
461	    print(f"\n  LLM calls:        {client.calls}")
462	    print(f"  Input tokens:     {client.total_input}")
463	    print(f"  Output tokens:    {client.total_output}")
464	    print(f"  Reasoning tokens: {client.total_reasoning}")

1	"""
2	Production-scale wiki compose validation against REAL evidence.
3	
4	Loads PG_TEST_041 (1024 LLM-extracted evidence pieces from a real Qwen 3.5
5	Plus production run on PFAS water filtration), runs build_wiki on it, then
6	composes the full report via the OpenAI shim using gpt-4o.
7	
8	This validates the wiki compose path at production scale (12 sections,
9	1024 evidence, 81 sources) using real prior-production evidence.
10	
11	WHAT THIS PROVES:
12	- Wiki builder handles 1024 evidence + 12 sections without error
13	- compose_from_wiki() scales to 12 sections without timeout / OOM
14	- Quality gate evaluates correctly at production scale
15	- Citation integrity holds with 81 sources
16	- Bibliography assembles correctly
17	- Final report > 8K words with > 50 citations
18	
19	WHAT THIS DOES NOT PROVE:
20	- G-Eval quality vs Qwen 3.5 Plus baseline (gpt-4o is comparable but different)
21	- Real LLM evidence extraction (this loads pre-extracted evidence)
22	- Full search → fetch → extract → wiki → compose end-to-end
23	"""
24	import asyncio
25	import json
26	import os
27	import re
28	import sys
29	import time
30	from pathlib import Path
31	
32	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
33	
34	from dotenv import load_dotenv
35	load_dotenv()
36	
37	os.environ["PG_WIKI_ENABLED"] = "1"
38	os.environ["PG_WIKI_5LENS"] = "1"
39	
40	import logging
41	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(message)s")
42	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
43	
44	# Reuse the OpenAI shim from the smaller test
45	from scripts.pg_compose_openai_validation import OpenAIShimClient
46	
47	
48	async def main():
49	    if not os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"):
50	        print("ERROR: OPENAI_API_KEY not set")
51	        return 1
52	
53	    model_name = os.getenv("OPENAI_TEST_MODEL", "gpt-4o")
54	    input_arg = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "outputs/polaris_graph/PG_TEST_041.json"
55	    output_id = os.getenv("OUTPUT_ID", "PRODUCTION_SCALE_VALIDATION")
56	
57	    print("=" * 70)
58	    print(f"PRODUCTION-SCALE WIKI COMPOSE — {model_name}")
59	    print(f"Source: {input_arg}")
60	    print("=" * 70)
61	
62	    # ── Stage 1: Load real evidence + outline ────────────────────────
63	    test_path = Path(input_arg)
64	    if not test_path.exists():
65	        print(f"ERROR: {test_path} not found")
66	        return 1
67	
68	    print(f"\n[1/4] Loading {test_path.name}")
69	    with open(test_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
70	        d = json.load(f)
71	
72	    evidence = d["evidence"]
73	    section_outline = d.get("section_outline", [])
74	    query = d.get("original_query", "")
75	
76	    # Convert section_outline to wiki builder format
77	    outline = [
78	        {
79	            "section_id": s.get("section_id", ""),
80	            "title": s.get("title", ""),
81	            "description": s.get("description", s.get("title", "")),
82	        }
83	        for s in section_outline
84	    ]
85	
86	    from collections import Counter
87	    tier_counts = Counter(e.get("quality_tier", "?") for e in evidence)
88	    print(f"  Query:    {query[:80]}")
89	    print(f"  Evidence: {len(evidence)} pieces ({dict(tier_counts)})")
90	    print(f"  Sources:  {len({e.get('source_url','') for e in evidence})} unique")
91	    print(f"  Sections: {len(outline)}")
92	
93	    # ── Stage 2: Build wiki from real evidence ───────────────────────
94	    print(f"\n[2/4] Building wiki via build_wiki()")
95	    from src.polaris_graph.wiki.wiki_builder import build_wiki
96	
97	    start = time.monotonic()
98	    wiki = build_wiki(
99	        evidence=evidence, outline=outline,
100	        query=query, vector_id=output_id,
101	    )
102	    build_elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
103	
104	    # The builder may have augmented the outline with synthesis sections.
105	    # Use that augmented outline for compose so the new sections are written.
106	    compose_outline = wiki.outline if wiki.outline else outline
107	
108	    total_claims = sum(len(c) for c in wiki.section_claims.values())
109	    print(f"  Built in {build_elapsed:.1f}s: {total_claims} claims, "
110	          f"{len(wiki.bibliography)} bib entries (outline: {len(compose_outline)} sections)")
111	    for sid, claims in wiki.section_claims.items():
112	        sec_title = next((s["title"] for s in compose_outline if s["section_id"] == sid), sid)
113	        srcs = len({c.get("source_url") for c in claims})
114	        print(f"    {sid}: {len(claims):3d} claims, {srcs:2d} sources | {sec_title[:42]}")
115	
116	    # ── Stage 3: Compose via OpenAI shim ────────────────────────────
117	    print(f"\n[3/4] Composing {len(compose_outline)} sections via {model_name}")
118	    from src.polaris_graph.wiki.wiki_composer import compose_from_wiki
119	
120	    client = OpenAIShimClient(model=model_name)
121	    start = time.monotonic()
122	    try:
123	        result = await compose_from_wiki(
124	            client=client,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
125	            wiki_result=wiki,
126	            query=query,
127	            outline=compose_outline,
128	        )
129	    except Exception as exc:
130	        print(f"\nFAIL: compose_from_wiki crashed: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
131	        await client.close()
132	        return 1
133	
134	    compose_elapsed = time.monotonic() - start
135	    await client.close()
136	
137	    # ── Stage 4: Inspect and validate ────────────────────────────────
138	    sections = result["sections"]
139	    qm = result["quality_metrics"]
140	    print(f"\n[4/4] Compose done in {compose_elapsed:.0f}s ({client.calls} LLM calls)")
141	    print(f"  Status:        {result['status']}")
142	    print(f"  Quality gate:  {result['quality_gate_result']}")
143	    print(f"  Sections:      {len(sections)}/{len(compose_outline)}")
144	    print(f"  Total words:   {qm['total_words']}")
145	    print(f"  Citations:     {qm['total_citations']}")
146	    print(f"  Sources used:  {qm['unique_sources']}")
147	    print(f"  Zero-cite sec: {qm['zero_cite_sections']}")
148	    print(f"  Avg cite/sec:  {qm['avg_citations_per_section']:.1f}")
149	    print()
150	    for s in sections:
151	        print(f"    {s['section_id']}: {s['word_count']:5d} words, "
152	              f"{len(s['citation_ids']):3d} cites — {s['title'][:40]}")
153	
154	    # ── Validation checks (production scale) ─────────────────────────
155	    print(f"\nValidation checks:")
156	    checks = []
157	
158	    v1 = len(sections) == len(compose_outline)
159	    checks.append(("V1 all sections composed", v1, f"{len(sections)}/{len(compose_outline)}"))
160	
161	    cot_phrases = ["let me", "i need to", "first, i'll", "thinking about",
162	                   "let's start", "i should", "i will write", "okay,",
163	                   "as an ai", "i'll write", "here's a"]
164	    cot_hits = sum(1 for s in sections for ph in cot_phrases
165	                   if ph in s["content"].lower())
166	    v2 = cot_hits == 0
167	    checks.append(("V2 no CoT leakage", v2, f"{cot_hits} hits"))
168	
169	    leftover_pattern = re.compile(r"\[(?:REF|CITE|Ref|Cite|ref|cite):\d+\]")
170	    leftover = sum(1 for s in sections if leftover_pattern.search(s["content"]))
171	    if leftover_pattern.search(result.get("final_report", "")):
172	        leftover += 1
173	    v3 = leftover == 0
174	    checks.append(("V3 citation prefixes resolved", v3, f"{leftover} leaks"))
175	
176	    placeholder_hits = sum(1 for s in sections if re.search(r"\[N\]", s["content"]))
177	    if re.search(r"\[N\]", result.get("final_report", "")):
178	        placeholder_hits += 1
179	    v3b = placeholder_hits == 0
180	    checks.append(("V3b no [N] placeholder", v3b, f"{placeholder_hits} leaks"))
181	
182	    avg_density = (qm["total_citations"] / qm["total_words"] * 100) if qm["total_words"] else 0
183	    ceiling = (qm["unique_sources"] / qm["total_words"] * 100) if qm["total_words"] else 0
184	    target = ceiling * 0.5  # at production scale, 50% of ceiling is fine
185	    v4 = avg_density >= target
186	    checks.append(("V4 citation density vs pool",
187	                   v4, f"{avg_density:.2f}/100w (target {target:.2f}, ceiling {ceiling:.2f})"))
188	
189	    bib_refs = set(b["ref_num"] for b in wiki.bibliography)
190	    orphans = sum(1 for s in sections
191	                  for n in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", s["content"])
192	                  if int(n) not in bib_refs)
193	    v5 = orphans == 0
194	    checks.append(("V5 no orphan citations", v5, f"{orphans} orphans"))
195	
196	    final = result["final_report"]
197	    v6 = "## References" in final and "[1]" in final and len(final) > 5000
198	    checks.append(("V6 final report assembled", v6, f"{len(final)} chars"))
199	
200	    # Production quality gate: 8K+ words for a multi-section systematic review
201	    v7 = qm["total_words"] >= 8000
202	    checks.append(("V7 word count >= 8K (prod gate)", v7, f"{qm['total_words']} words"))
203	
204	    # Production: 50+ citations across all sections
205	    v8 = qm["total_citations"] >= 50
206	    checks.append(("V8 total citations >= 50", v8, f"{qm['total_citations']} cites"))
207	
208	    # Production: every section has citations
209	    v9 = qm["zero_cite_sections"] == 0
210	    checks.append(("V9 zero zero-cite sections", v9,
211	                   f"{qm['zero_cite_sections']} sections w/o cites"))
212	
213	    print()
214	    all_pass = True
215	    for name, ok, detail in checks:
216	        marker = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
217	        if not ok:
218	            all_pass = False
219	        print(f"  [{marker}] {name:35s} {detail}")
220	
221	    # ── Save the final report ───────────────────────────────────────
222	    out_dir = Path("outputs/polaris_graph")
223	    out_file = out_dir / f"{output_id}.md"
224	    out_file.write_text(final, encoding="utf-8")
225	    print(f"\nReport saved: {out_file} ({len(final)} chars)")
226	
227	    # Also save the full result as JSON for downstream evaluation (G-Eval, etc.)
228	    json_file = out_dir / f"{output_id}.json"
229	    json_blob = {
230	        "vector_id": output_id,
231	        "original_query": query,
232	        "final_report": final,
233	        "sections": sections,
234	        "bibliography": wiki.bibliography,
235	        "quality_metrics": qm,
236	        "section_outline": result["section_outline"],
237	        "evidence_chain": result["evidence_chain"],
238	        "model": model_name,
239	        "compose_seconds": round(compose_elapsed, 1),
240	    }
241	    json_file.write_text(json.dumps(json_blob, indent=2, default=str), encoding="utf-8")
242	    print(f"JSON saved:   {json_file}")
243	
244	    # ── Cost ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
245	    pricing = {
246	        "gpt-5":         (1.25, 10.00),
247	        "gpt-4o":        (2.50, 10.00),
248	        "gpt-4o-mini":   (0.15,  0.60),
249	        "gpt-4-turbo":   (10.00, 30.00),
250	    }
251	    in_p, out_p = pricing.get(model_name, (2.50, 10.00))
252	    cost = (client.total_input / 1_000_000 * in_p) + (client.total_output / 1_000_000 * out_p)
253	    print(f"\n  LLM calls:        {client.calls}")
254	    print(f"  Input tokens:     {client.total_input:,}")
255	    print(f"  Output tokens:    {client.total_output:,}")
256	    print(f"  Cost:             ${cost:.4f}")
257	
258	    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
259	    print("RESULT: " + ("ALL PASS" if all_pass else "SOME FAILED"))
260	    print(f"  Build: {build_elapsed:.1f}s | Compose: {compose_elapsed:.0f}s")
261	    print(f"  {len(sections)} sections, {qm['total_words']} words, "
262	          f"{qm['total_citations']} citations, {qm['unique_sources']} sources")
263	    print("=" * 70)
264	
265	    return 0 if all_pass else 1
266	
267	
268	if __name__ == "__main__":
269	    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))

1	"""
2	G-Eval style LLM-as-Judge evaluation using the OpenAI shim.
3	
4	Substitutes OpenAI gpt-4o for the OpenRouter→Qwen path so we can score
5	reports while OpenRouter is 402 blocked. Same 6 dimensions, same prompts,
6	same weights as scripts/eval_geval.py — only the client is different.
7	
8	USAGE:
9	    python scripts/pg_geval_openai.py outputs/polaris_graph/PRODUCTION_SCALE_VALIDATION.json
10	"""
11	import asyncio
12	import json
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import re
16	import sys
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
20	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
21	os.chdir(PROJECT_ROOT)
22	
23	from dotenv import load_dotenv
24	load_dotenv()
25	
26	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(message)s")
27	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
28	
29	from scripts.pg_compose_openai_validation import OpenAIShimClient
30	
31	MAX_EVAL_CHARS = 30000
32	
33	
34	async def _eval_one(client, dim_name, prompt, system, weight):
35	    try:
36	        response = await asyncio.wait_for(
37	            client.generate(prompt=prompt, system=system, max_tokens=1024, temperature=0.3),
38	            timeout=180,
39	        )
40	        text = response.content.strip()
41	        score_match = re.search(r"SCORE:\s*(\d+)", text)
42	        score = int(score_match.group(1)) if score_match else 5
43	        reason_match = re.search(r"REASONING:\s*(.+?)(?=ISSUES:|$)", text, re.DOTALL)
44	        reasoning = reason_match.group(1).strip() if reason_match else text[:300]
45	        issues_match = re.search(r"ISSUES:\s*(.+?)$", text, re.DOTALL)
46	        issues = issues_match.group(1).strip() if issues_match else ""
47	        return {
48	            "score": score, "weight": weight,
49	            "weighted": round(score * weight, 2),
50	            "reasoning": reasoning[:400], "issues": issues[:400],
51	        }
52	    except Exception as exc:
53	        logger.error(f"  {dim_name}: FAILED - {str(exc)[:100]}")
54	        return {"score": 0, "weight": weight, "weighted": 0, "error": str(exc)[:200]}
55	
56	
57	async def evaluate_report(report_path: str):
58	    with open(report_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
59	        data = json.load(f)
60	
61	    report_text = data.get("final_report", "")
62	    sections = data.get("sections", data.get("report_sections", []))
63	    bibliography = data.get("bibliography", [])
64	    query = data.get("original_query", "research topic")
65	
66	    if not report_text:
67	        logger.error("No final_report in JSON")
68	        return
69	
70	    logger.info(f"Loaded {report_path}")
71	    logger.info(f"  Query:    {query[:70]}")
72	    logger.info(f"  Sections: {len(sections)}")
73	    logger.info(f"  Bib:      {len(bibliography)}")
74	    logger.info(f"  Report:   {len(report_text)} chars")
75	
76	    model = os.getenv("GEVAL_MODEL", "gpt-4o")
77	    client = OpenAIShimClient(model=model)
78	    logger.info(f"  Judge:    {model}")
79	    logger.info("")
80	
81	    results = {}
82	    total_weighted = 0.0
83	    system = "You are an expert research report evaluator. Be critical and specific. Do not inflate scores."
84	
85	    # 1. FAITHFULNESS — per-section
86	    logger.info("Evaluating FAITHFULNESS (per-section)...")
87	    faith_scores = []
88	    for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
89	        title = sec.get("title", "")
90	        content = sec.get("content", "")[:8000]
91	        if len(content.split()) < 50:
92	            continue
93	        prompt = f"""Evaluate FAITHFULNESS of this research report section.
94	
95	For each factual claim, check: is it supported by a citation [N]?
96	Are numbers and effect sizes accurately attributed?
97	
98	Score 1-10:
99	- 10: Every claim cited, numbers match
100	- 7-9: Most claims cited, minor gaps
101	- 4-6: Multiple uncited factual claims
102	- 1-3: Widespread unsupported claims
103	
104	SECTION "{title}":
105	{content}
106	
107	Output: SCORE: [1-10]
108	REASONING: [2-3 sentences]
109	ISSUES: [Specific uncited claims, or "None found"]"""
110	        r = await _eval_one(client, f"faith_{i}", prompt, system, 0.20)
111	        faith_scores.append(r["score"])
112	        logger.info(f"  Section {i+1} '{title[:40]}': {r['score']}/10")
113	
114	    avg_faith = sum(faith_scores) / max(len(faith_scores), 1)
115	    results["faithfulness"] = {
116	        "score": round(avg_faith), "weight": 0.20,
117	        "weighted": round(avg_faith * 0.20, 2),
118	        "reasoning": f"Average across {len(faith_scores)} sections: {avg_faith:.1f}/10",
119	        "per_section": faith_scores,
120	    }
121	    total_weighted += avg_faith * 0.20
122	    logger.info(f"  FAITHFULNESS average: {avg_faith:.1f}/10\n")
123	
124	    # 2. COHERENCE
125	    logger.info("Evaluating COHERENCE...")
126	    structure = []
127	    for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
128	        content = sec.get("content", "")
129	        sents = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+", content)
130	        first = sents[0][:250] if sents else ""
131	        last = sents[-1][:250] if len(sents) > 1 else ""
132	        structure.append(
133	            f"Section {i+1}: {sec.get('title','')}\n"
134	            f"  Words: {len(content.split())}\n"
135	            f"  Opening: {first}\n"
136	            f"  Closing: {last}"
137	        )
138	    prompt = f"""Evaluate COHERENCE of this research report structure.
139	
140	Check: logical flow between sections, transition sentences, narrative arc,
141	cross-references, no abrupt topic jumps.
142	
143	Score 1-10:
144	- 10: Perfect flow, smooth transitions
145	- 7-9: Good flow, minor gaps
146	- 4-6: Fragmented, weak transitions
147	- 1-3: Incoherent
148	
149	REPORT STRUCTURE ({len(sections)} sections):
150	{chr(10).join(structure)}
151	
152	Output: SCORE: [1-10]
153	REASONING: [2-3 sentences]
154	ISSUES: [Specific flow problems, or "None found"]"""
155	    r = await _eval_one(client, "coherence", prompt, system, 0.15)
156	    results["coherence"] = r
157	    total_weighted += r["weighted"]
158	    logger.info(f"  COHERENCE: {r['score']}/10\n")
159	
160	    # 3. ANALYTICAL DEPTH
161	    logger.info("Evaluating ANALYTICAL DEPTH...")
162	    sorted_secs = sorted(sections, key=lambda s: len(s.get("content", "")), reverse=True)
163	    sample_text = "\n\n---\n\n".join(
164	        f"## {s.get('title','')}\n{s.get('content','')[:5000]}"
165	        for s in sorted_secs[:3]
166	    )
167	    prompt = f"""Evaluate ANALYTICAL DEPTH of this research report (3 largest sections shown).
168	
169	A deep report: COMPARES studies, EXPLAINS mechanisms, IDENTIFIES contradictions,
170	CONTEXTUALIZES with practical significance, uses quantitative measures with CIs.
171	
172	Score 1-10:
173	- 10: Deep synthesis with comparisons, mechanisms, contradictions, quantitative grading
174	- 7-9: Good analysis, most elements present
175	- 4-6: Mostly descriptive
176	- 1-3: Pure listing
177	
178	REPORT SAMPLE:
179	{sample_text[:MAX_EVAL_CHARS]}
180	
181	Output: SCORE: [1-10]
182	REASONING: [2-3 sentences]
183	ISSUES: [Where analysis is shallow, or "None found"]"""
184	    r = await _eval_one(client, "analytical_depth", prompt, system, 0.20)
185	    results["analytical_depth"] = r
186	    total_weighted += r["weighted"]
187	    logger.info(f"  ANALYTICAL DEPTH: {r['score']}/10\n")
188	
189	    # 4. CITATION QUALITY
190	    logger.info("Evaluating CITATION QUALITY...")
191	    cite_summary = []
192	    for i, sec in enumerate(sections):
193	        content = sec.get("content", "")
194	        n_cites = len(re.findall(r"\[\d+\]", content))
195	        n_words = len(content.split())
196	        cite_summary.append(f"  Section {i+1} '{sec.get('title','')[:40]}': {n_words}w, {n_cites} citations")
197	
198	    bib_text = "\n".join(
199	        f"  [{b.get('ref_num', i+1)}] {b.get('title', b.get('formatted','')[:100])}"
200	        f" | {b.get('url','')[:60]}"
201	        for i, b in enumerate(bibliography[:25])
202	    )
203	
204	    prompt = f"""Evaluate CITATION QUALITY of this research report.
205	
206	Check: citation diversity, placement, bibliography completeness,
207	uncited claims, balance across sections.
208	
209	CITATION DISTRIBUTION:
210	{chr(10).join(cite_summary)}
211	
212	BIBLIOGRAPHY ({len(bibliography)} entries):
213	{bib_text}
214	
215	Score 1-10:
216	- 10: Diverse, well-placed, complete bibliography, balanced
217	- 7-9: Good with minor gaps
218	- 4-6: Clustering, missing citations, poor bibliography
219	- 1-3: Sparse or absent
220	
221	Output: SCORE: [1-10]
222	REASONING: [2-3 sentences]
223	ISSUES: [Specific problems, or "None found"]"""
224	    r = await _eval_one(client, "citation_quality", prompt, system, 0.15)
225	    results["citation_quality"] = r
226	    total_weighted += r["weighted"]
227	    logger.info(f"  CITATION QUALITY: {r['score']}/10\n")
228	
229	    # 5. COMPLETENESS — uses query as topic (NOT hardcoded)
230	    logger.info("Evaluating COMPLETENESS...")
231	    section_list = "\n".join(
232	        f"  {i+1}. {s.get('title','')} ({len(s.get('content','').split())} words)"
233	        for i, s in enumerate(sections)
234	    )
235	    later_sample = "\n".join(
236	        s.get("content", "")[:2000]
237	        for s in sections[len(sections)//2:]
238	    )
239	    prompt = f"""Evaluate COMPLETENESS of this systematic review answering: "{query}"
240	
241	A complete systematic review covers: the underlying problem (mechanism, causes,
242	significance), the available solution approaches/technologies, evidence on
243	effectiveness, limitations and trade-offs, comparative analysis, practical
244	implementation considerations, and remaining knowledge gaps.
245	
246	REPORT SECTIONS:
247	{section_list}
248	
249	SAMPLE FROM LATER SECTIONS:
250	{later_sample[:10000]}
251	
252	Score 1-10:
253	- 10: All major topics for this query are covered with evidence
254	- 7-9: Most covered, minor gaps
255	- 4-6: Several important topics missing
256	- 1-3: Major gaps
257	
258	Output: SCORE: [1-10]
259	REASONING: [2-3 sentences]
260	ISSUES: [Missing topics, or "None found"]"""
261	    r = await _eval_one(client, "completeness", prompt, system, 0.15)
262	    results["completeness"] = r
263	    total_weighted += r["weighted"]
264	    logger.info(f"  COMPLETENESS: {r['score']}/10\n")
265	
266	    # 6. WRITING QUALITY
267	    logger.info("Evaluating WRITING QUALITY...")
268	    samples = []
269	    for idx in [0, min(2, len(sections)-1), len(sections)-1]:
270	        if idx < len(sections):
271	            c = sections[idx].get("content", "")
272	            samples.append(c[:3000])
273	    writing_sample = "\n\n---\n\n".join(samples)
274	
275	    prompt = f"""Evaluate WRITING QUALITY of this research report.
276	
277	Check: academic register, grammar (subject-verb agreement, modals),
278	no AI artifacts, appropriate hedging, clear language, effective structure.
279	
280	Score 1-10:
281	- 10: Publication-ready
282	- 7-9: Strong with minor issues
283	- 4-6: Noticeable errors
284	- 1-3: Poor writing
285	
286	REPORT SAMPLES (from sections 1, 3, and last):
287	{writing_sample[:MAX_EVAL_CHARS]}
288	
289	Output: SCORE: [1-10]
290	REASONING: [2-3 sentences]
291	ISSUES: [Specific problems, or "None found"]"""
292	    r = await _eval_one(client, "writing_quality", prompt, system, 0.15)
293	    results["writing_quality"] = r
294	    total_weighted += r["weighted"]
295	    logger.info(f"  WRITING QUALITY: {r['score']}/10\n")
296	
297	    await client.close()
298	
299	    # TOTAL
300	    total = round(total_weighted * 10, 1)
301	    logger.info("=" * 60)
302	    logger.info(f"  G-EVAL TOTAL: {total}/100")
303	    logger.info("=" * 60)
304	    for dim, r in results.items():
305	        if dim == "faithfulness":
306	            logger.info(f"  {dim:20s} {r['score']}/10  weight={r['weight']}  contrib={r['weighted']}")
307	        else:
308	            logger.info(f"  {dim:20s} {r['score']}/10  weight={r['weight']}  contrib={r['weighted']}")
309	
310	    output_path = report_path.replace(".json", "_geval_openai.json")
311	    with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
312	        json.dump({
313	            "total": total,
314	            "judge_model": model,
315	            "dimensions": results,
316	            "evaluation_meta": {
317	                "input_report": str(report_path),
318	                "query": query,
319	                "sections_evaluated": len(sections),
320	                "bib_entries": len(bibliography),
321	            },
322	        }, f, indent=2)
323	    logger.info(f"  Saved to: {output_path}")
324	
325	    return total, results
326	
327	
328	if __name__ == "__main__":
329	    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
330	        print("Usage: python scripts/pg_geval_openai.py <report.json>")
331	        sys.exit(1)
332	    asyncio.run(evaluate_report(sys.argv[1]))

1	"""
2	Mesh Preflight -- side-by-side model comparison on real data.
3	
4	Tests both GLM 5.1 and Qwen 3.6 Plus against the same sources,
5	comparing: extraction quality, entity yield, JSON compliance,
6	edge formation, retrieval relevance, composition citations.
7	
8	Usage:
9	    python scripts/pg_mesh_preflight.py
10	
11	Requires: OPENROUTER_API_KEY in .env
12	"""
13	
14	import asyncio
15	import logging
16	import sys
17	import time
18	from pathlib import Path
19	
20	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
21	
22	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
23	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh import MeshStore
24	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.claim_extract import extract_claims_from_source
25	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.compose.composer import compose_answer
26	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.edge_discovery import discover_edges_for_claims
27	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.ingest import ingest_file
28	from src.polaris_graph.wiki.mesh.retrieve.lethal import lethal_retrieve
29	
30	logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING)
31	logger = logging.getLogger("preflight")
32	logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
33	
34	# ----- test sources -----
35	
36	SOURCE_A = """# GAC Filtration for PFAS Removal
37	
38	Granular activated carbon (GAC) filtration is widely used for removing per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) from drinking water. In a 12-month study across 15 municipal treatment plants, GAC achieved an average removal efficiency of 85% for long-chain PFAS compounds including PFOS and PFOA, with contact times standardized at 10 minutes per treatment cycle.
39	
40	The effectiveness of GAC varies significantly by PFAS chain length. Long-chain compounds (C8 and above) showed removal rates of 85-95%, while short-chain PFAS (C4-C6) like PFBS showed substantially lower removal at 40-60%. The carbon bed lifetime before breakthrough averaged 18 months for long-chain compounds but only 6-8 months for short-chain variants.
41	
42	Cost analysis indicates GAC treatment adds approximately $0.15-0.25 per 1000 gallons of treated water, making it one of the more cost-effective PFAS removal technologies available for municipal-scale deployment. However, spent carbon disposal and regeneration costs can add 30-40% to the total lifecycle cost.
43	
44	The EPA has recommended GAC as one of the best available technologies (BAT) for PFAS removal under the proposed Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCLs) of 4 parts per trillion for PFOS and PFOA individually."""
45	
46	SOURCE_B = """# Reverse Osmosis for PFAS Treatment
47	
48	Reverse osmosis (RO) membrane technology demonstrates superior PFAS removal performance compared to adsorption-based methods. A multi-site study involving 42 independent measurements across 8 water utilities found RO membranes achieved 95% or greater removal of all tested PFAS compounds, regardless of chain length.
49	
50	The key advantage of RO over GAC is its effectiveness against short-chain PFAS compounds. While GAC removal drops to 40-60% for short-chain PFAS, RO maintains greater than 90% removal efficiency across all chain lengths tested (C4 through C14). This makes RO the preferred technology for water sources contaminated with mixed-chain-length PFAS.
51	
52	However, RO generates a concentrated reject stream containing 15-25% of the input water volume, creating a secondary waste management challenge. The reject stream requires additional treatment or disposal, adding $0.40-0.60 per 1000 gallons to the total treatment cost.
53	
54	Energy consumption for RO treatment averages 3-5 kWh per 1000 gallons, compared to negligible energy requirements for GAC. This energy penalty, combined with higher capital costs, makes RO approximately 2-3 times more expensive than GAC for large-scale municipal deployment."""
55	
56	MODELS = [
57	    ("Qwen 3.5 Plus", "qwen/qwen3.5-plus-02-15"),
58	    ("GLM 5.1", "z-ai/glm-5.1"),
59	    ("Claude Sonnet 4.5", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5"),
60	]
61	
62	QUESTION = "What are the most effective methods for removing PFAS from drinking water, and how do they compare on cost and performance?"
63	
64	
65	# ----- runner -----
66	
67	async def run_model_test(model_name: str, model_id: str, base_dir: Path):
68	    """Run the full pipeline with one model and return metrics."""
69	    logger.info("=" * 60)
70	    logger.info("TESTING: %s (%s)", model_name, model_id)
71	    logger.info("=" * 60)
72	
73	    db_path = base_dir / f"{model_name.replace(' ', '_').lower()}.db"
74	    store = MeshStore.open(db_path)
75	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model_id)
76	
77	    metrics = {
78	        "model": model_name,
79	        "model_id": model_id,
80	        "errors": [],
81	    }
82	
83	    try:
84	        # -- Step 1: Create workspace + ingest --
85	        ws_id = store.create_workspace(
86	            name=f"Preflight {model_name}",
87	            root_question=QUESTION,
88	        )
89	
90	        file_a = base_dir / "source_a.md"
91	        file_a.write_text(SOURCE_A, encoding="utf-8")
92	        file_b = base_dir / "source_b.md"
93	        file_b.write_text(SOURCE_B, encoding="utf-8")
94	
95	        src_a, _ = ingest_file(
96	            store=store, workspace_id=ws_id,
97	            file_path=file_a, kind="upload",
98	            url="https://example.com/gac-study",
99	        )
100	        src_b, _ = ingest_file(
101	            store=store, workspace_id=ws_id,
102	            file_path=file_b, kind="web",
103	            url="https://example.com/ro-study",
104	        )
105	        metrics["sources_ingested"] = 2
106	
107	        # -- Step 2: Extract claims --
108	        t0 = time.time()
109	        try:
110	            result_a = await extract_claims_from_source(
111	                client=client, store=store,
112	                workspace_id=ws_id, source_page_id=src_a,
113	                query=QUESTION,
114	            )
115	            result_b = await extract_claims_from_source(
116	                client=client, store=store,
117	                workspace_id=ws_id, source_page_id=src_b,
118	                query=QUESTION,
119	            )
120	            extract_time = time.time() - t0
121	            metrics["extract_time_s"] = round(extract_time, 1)
122	            metrics["claims_a"] = len(result_a.inserted_claim_ids)
123	            metrics["claims_b"] = len(result_b.inserted_claim_ids)
124	            metrics["total_claims"] = metrics["claims_a"] + metrics["claims_b"]
125	            metrics["skipped_a"] = result_a.skipped
126	            metrics["skipped_b"] = result_b.skipped
127	            metrics["facts_seen_a"] = result_a.total_facts_seen
128	            metrics["facts_seen_b"] = result_b.total_facts_seen
129	
130	            all_claim_ids = result_a.inserted_claim_ids + result_b.inserted_claim_ids
131	
132	            # Collect rejected quotes for diagnostics
133	            all_rejected = (
134	                getattr(result_a, "rejected_quotes", [])
135	                + getattr(result_b, "rejected_quotes", [])
136	            )
137	            metrics["rejected_quotes"] = sorted(all_rejected, key=lambda x: x[0])[:10]
138	        except Exception as exc:
139	            metrics["errors"].append(f"EXTRACTION FAILED: {exc}")
140	            logger.error("Extraction failed for %s: %s", model_name, exc)
141	            return metrics
142	
143	        # -- Step 3: Check entities --
144	        entities = store._conn.execute(
145	            "SELECT canonical_name, entity_type FROM entities "
146	            "WHERE workspace_id = ?", (ws_id,),
147	        ).fetchall()
148	        metrics["entity_count"] = len(entities)
149	        metrics["entity_names"] = [e["canonical_name"] for e in entities]
150	        metrics["entity_types"] = {
151	            e["canonical_name"]: e["entity_type"] for e in entities
152	        }
153	
154	        # -- Step 4: Discover edges --
155	        edge_result = discover_edges_for_claims(
156	            store, workspace_id=ws_id,
157	            new_claim_ids=all_claim_ids,
158	        )
159	        metrics["edges_total"] = len(edge_result.edge_ids)
160	        metrics["edges_corroborate"] = edge_result.corroboration_count
161	        metrics["edges_contradict"] = edge_result.contradiction_count
162	
163	        # -- Step 5: Retrieve --
164	        retrieval = lethal_retrieve(
165	            store, workspace_id=ws_id,
166	            question_text=QUESTION,
167	        )
168	        metrics["retrieved_claims"] = len(retrieval.scored_claims)
169	        metrics["gap_category"] = retrieval.gap_category
170	        if retrieval.scored_claims:
171	            metrics["top_score"] = round(retrieval.scored_claims[0][1], 4)
172	            top_claim = store.get_claim(retrieval.scored_claims[0][0])
173	            metrics["top_claim"] = top_claim["statement"][:100] if top_claim else "?"
174	
175	        # -- Step 6: Compose --
176	        t1 = time.time()
177	        try:
178	            compose_result = await compose_answer(
179	                client, store,
180	                workspace_id=ws_id,
181	                retrieval_result=retrieval,
182	                question_text=QUESTION,
183	            )
184	            compose_time = time.time() - t1
185	            metrics["compose_time_s"] = round(compose_time, 1)
186	            metrics["answer_length"] = len(compose_result.answer_text)
187	            metrics["answer_words"] = len(compose_result.answer_text.split())
188	            metrics["bibliography_count"] = len(compose_result.bibliography)
189	
190	            # Count citations
191	            import re
192	            citations = re.findall(r"\[\d+\]", compose_result.answer_text)
193	            metrics["citation_count"] = len(citations)
194	            unique_refs = set(citations)
195	            metrics["unique_citations"] = len(unique_refs)
196	
197	            # Check for CoT leakage
198	            cot_markers = ["<think>", "<reasoning>", "**Planning", "**Step"]
199	            has_cot = any(m in compose_result.answer_text for m in cot_markers)
200	            metrics["cot_leakage"] = has_cot
201	
202	            metrics["answer_preview"] = compose_result.answer_text[:300]
203	        except Exception as exc:
204	            metrics["errors"].append(f"COMPOSITION FAILED: {exc}")
205	            logger.error("Composition failed for %s: %s", model_name, exc)
206	
207	        # -- Step 7: Claim quality sample --
208	        claims = []
209	        for cid in all_claim_ids[:5]:
210	            c = store.get_claim(cid)
211	            if c:
212	                claims.append({
213	                    "statement": c["statement"][:80],
214	                    "tier": c["tier"],
215	                    "has_numeric": bool(c["has_numeric"]),
216	                    "relevance": c["relevance_score"],
217	                })
218	        metrics["sample_claims"] = claims
219	
220	    except Exception as exc:
221	        metrics["errors"].append(f"PIPELINE FAILED: {exc}")
222	        logger.error("Pipeline failed for %s: %s", model_name, exc)
223	    finally:
224	        await client.close()
225	        store.close()
226	
227	    return metrics
228	
229	
230	def print_comparison(results: list[dict]):
231	    """Print a side-by-side comparison of model results."""
232	    print("\n" + "=" * 70)
233	    print("MESH PREFLIGHT -- MODEL COMPARISON")
234	    print("=" * 70)
235	
236	    for r in results:
237	        print(f"\n{'-' * 70}")
238	        print(f"MODEL: {r['model']} ({r['model_id']})")
239	        print(f"{'-' * 70}")
240	
241	        if r.get("errors"):
242	            for err in r["errors"]:
243	                print(f"  *** {err}")
244	
245	        print(f"\n  EXTRACTION:")
246	        print(f"    Time:           {r.get('extract_time_s', '?')}s")
247	        print(f"    Facts seen:     A={r.get('facts_seen_a', '?')}, B={r.get('facts_seen_b', '?')}")
248	        print(f"    Claims inserted: A={r.get('claims_a', '?')}, B={r.get('claims_b', '?')} (total: {r.get('total_claims', '?')})")
249	        print(f"    Skipped A:      {r.get('skipped_a', '?')}")
250	        print(f"    Skipped B:      {r.get('skipped_b', '?')}")
251	
252	        print(f"\n  ENTITIES ({r.get('entity_count', 0)}):")
253	        for name in r.get("entity_names", [])[:10]:
254	            etype = r.get("entity_types", {}).get(name, "?")
255	            print(f"    {name} ({etype})")
256	
257	        print(f"\n  EDGES:")
258	        print(f"    Total:          {r.get('edges_total', 0)}")
259	        print(f"    Corroborate:    {r.get('edges_corroborate', 0)}")
260	        print(f"    Contradict:     {r.get('edges_contradict', 0)}")
261	
262	        print(f"\n  RETRIEVAL:")
263	        print(f"    Claims found:   {r.get('retrieved_claims', 0)}")
264	        print(f"    Gap category:   {r.get('gap_category', '?')}")
265	        print(f"    Top score:      {r.get('top_score', '?')}")
266	        print(f"    Top claim:      {r.get('top_claim', '?')}")
267	
268	        print(f"\n  COMPOSITION:")
269	        print(f"    Time:           {r.get('compose_time_s', '?')}s")
270	        print(f"    Words:          {r.get('answer_words', '?')}")
271	        print(f"    Citations:      {r.get('citation_count', '?')} ({r.get('unique_citations', '?')} unique)")
272	        print(f"    Bibliography:   {r.get('bibliography_count', '?')} entries")
273	        print(f"    CoT leakage:    {'YES WARNING' if r.get('cot_leakage') else 'No OK'}")
274	
275	        print(f"\n  ANSWER PREVIEW:")
276	        preview = r.get("answer_preview", "(none)")
277	        for line in preview.split("\n")[:5]:
278	            print(f"    {line[:80]}")
279	
280	        rejected = r.get("rejected_quotes", [])
281	        if rejected:
282	            print(f"\n  REJECTED QUOTES (shortest {len(rejected)}):")
283	            for word_count, text in rejected[:5]:
284	                print(f"    [{word_count} words] {text}")
285	
286	        print(f"\n  SAMPLE CLAIMS:")
287	        for c in r.get("sample_claims", []):
288	            print(f"    [{c['tier']}] {'#' if c['has_numeric'] else '  '} "
289	                  f"rel={c['relevance']:.2f}  {c['statement']}")
290	
291	    # -- Summary --
292	    print(f"\n{'=' * 70}")
293	    print("SUMMARY")
294	    print(f"{'=' * 70}")
295	    for r in results:
296	        errs = len(r.get("errors", []))
297	        claims = r.get("total_claims", 0)
298	        ents = r.get("entity_count", 0)
299	        edges = r.get("edges_total", 0)
300	        cites = r.get("citation_count", 0)
301	        cot = "LEAK" if r.get("cot_leakage") else "clean"
302	        status = "FAIL" if errs > 0 else "PASS"
303	        print(f"  {r['model']:20s}  {status}  claims={claims}  "
304	              f"entities={ents}  edges={edges}  citations={cites}  "
305	              f"cot={cot}  errors={errs}")
306	
307	
308	async def main():
309	    import tempfile
310	    base_dir = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="mesh_preflight_"))
311	    logger.info("Working directory: %s", base_dir)
312	
313	    results = []
314	    for model_name, model_id in MODELS:
315	        try:
316	            metrics = await run_model_test(model_name, model_id, base_dir)
317	            results.append(metrics)
318	        except Exception as exc:
319	            logger.error("Fatal error testing %s: %s", model_name, exc)
320	            results.append({
321	                "model": model_name,
322	                "model_id": model_id,
323	                "errors": [f"FATAL: {exc}"],
324	            })
325	
326	    print_comparison(results)
327	
328	    any_fail = any(r.get("errors") for r in results)
329	    if any_fail:
330	        print("\nWARNING  SOME MODELS HAD ERRORS -- check output above")
331	    else:
332	        print("\nOK  ALL MODELS PASSED PREFLIGHT")
333	
334	    return 1 if any_fail else 0
335	
336	
337	if __name__ == "__main__":
338	    sys.exit(asyncio.run(main()))
339
